text.title: business-model-planning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for business-model-planning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Citing Cirque du Soleil, Yellow Tail wine, Nintendo Wii, or iTunes as Blue Ocean examples
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These are the 5-7 textbook cases cited because they succeeded. The failure rate of "create new factor" bets is high and invisible. Require post-2018 examples and reject the canonical textbook cases.

failure-mode.description: Running Blue Ocean analysis on a single niche without a comparison set
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Blue Ocean is only meaningful when contrasted against a current red ocean; without it you cannot identify what to eliminate or reduce.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends "tiered subscription" without naming 3 distinct WTP segments
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forces the agent to reason about actual buyer segments and job differences rather than defaulting to the industry template.

failure-mode.description: WTP estimates anchored to $19/$49/$99 with no external benchmark
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin to a ProfitWell category report or competitor scrape before letting the agent suggest tiers. Without a benchmark, LLMs regress to round numbers.
